conversation.content:
Extract values for these columns. Document has 0 pages.

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups with synchronous metastases, where synchronous metastases refers to metastases present at the time of diagnosis of prostate cancer. with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No;
---
Column 2: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups of patients with metachronous diseases (diseases that occur at different times). with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No
---
Column 3: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups with high volume/burden of metastases, with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No
---
Column 4: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
Definition: What indicates whether the results were reported based on prognostic groups having a low volume of metastasis, with a yes or no response (Y/N)? Answer Yes or No
---
Column 5: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing adverse events of all causes with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
---
Column 6: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
Definition: What number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the control arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
---
Column 7: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
Definition: What number and percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
---
Column 8: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Control
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing adverse events that are related to the treatment and have a severity grade of 3 or higher in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
---
Column 9: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients showing adverse events that are treatment-related and have a grade of 5 in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
---
Column 10: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Control
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 5 in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.

For informational columns (trial name, arms, etc.), use get_chunks_by_page([1, 2]) first. For specific columns, use search_chunks. Submit when you have enough information.
